title: betula pubescens subsp. concinna
intro: Betula pubescens subsp. concinna, also known as downy birch, is a subspecies of the birch tree (Betula pubescens) native to northern Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Betula pubescens subsp. concinna include reducing fever, alleviating headaches and digestive issues, and supporting immune system function. Herbal preparations of downy birch can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health complaints, and the bark can also be used to make a salve or ointment for topical application.
